NWS SURVEY DETERMINED A SMALL TORNADO OCCURRED JUST WEST OF BROWN HILL ROAD IN SUGAR GROVE TOWNSHIP MOVING EAST FOR A MINUTE BEFORE LIFTING UP WEST OF THE ROAD. THE SMALL EF0 TORNADO WAS EMBEDDED IN AN AREA OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WHERE ABOUT 100 TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN AND UPROOTED. ABOUT 4 HOMES AND A CHURCH HAD MINOR DAMAGE WHILE AN OUTBUILDING WAS DAMAGED AND ANOTHER ONE DESTROYED.
TREES DOWN ON ROUTE 62 NEAR BLUEBERRY GOLF COURSE. POWER OUTAGE FROM 920 PM TO 935 PM.

A NWS SURVEY DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS COLLAPSED A LIGHT POLE AND A FLAG POLE AND REMOVED A ROOF COVERING FROM A LARGE BUILDING AT A BASEBALL FIELD LOCATED JUST NORTH OF BACKUP CORNERS NEAR ROUTE 62 IN PINE GROVE TWP. MAX WIND SPEED GUSTS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. BLEACHERS...FENCING AND THE ROOF OF THE CONCESSION STAND WERE ALSO DAMAGED.
TREES AND WIRES DOWN ON ROUTE 62 AND COLONY DRIVE

NWS SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED IN PINE GROVE AND ELK TOWNSHIPS. THE TORNADO BEGAN WEST OF COLE HILL ROAD ABOUT 3 MILES SOUTHEAST OF RUSSELL AND MOVED EAST NORTHEAST WITH INTERMITTENT TOUCHDOWNS TO NEAR MACKS CORNERS BEFORE LIFTING. TWO HOMES HAD MINOR DAMAGE. ABOUT 500 TREES WERE SNAPPED OR DOWNED IN ABOUT A 4 MILE INTERMITTENT PATH.
